surface.name: Paddle Billing end-to-end sandbox Quickstart
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, Paddle’s end-to-end sandbox Quickstart is primary: there is no local or OSS Paddle billing service, and the docs identify sandbox as the recommended testing route. Signup is one opaque step at the official sandbox URL, followed by the complete public catalog, Paddle.js, checkout, and webhook sequence.

alternatives_considered.name: Backend SDK quickstarts
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Node.js, Python, Go, and PHP are peer SDK-specific first-request routes, while the selected Quickstart is explicitly the end-to-end integration.

alternatives_considered.name: Starter kits
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Next.js and web monetization kits start from working applications rather than the framework-agnostic walkthrough.

alternatives_considered.name: Paddle.js five-minute quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This narrower route stops at opening a first checkout, while the selected general Quickstart includes catalog, payment, and webhook handling.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Paddle sandbox account and complete the official end-to-end Quickstart until a second test checkout sends lifecycle webhooks to the configured Hookdeck destination.
assumed_prior_state: No Paddle sandbox or live account, catalog, product, price, client-side token, default payment link, notification destination, transaction, customer, subscription, or prior integration is assumed.
A browser and a personal and business identity suitable for sandbox signup are available; the downstream guide also uses the public Paddle CodePen and Hookdeck Console.
No account was created, details or terms were submitted, checkout was opened, payment was taken, webhook destination was created, or webhook was sent during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Complete the end-to-end Quickstart with a pricing page connected to Paddle Checkout and webhooks flowing for provisioning.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The intended terminal is a Paddle sandbox pricing page that completes a test checkout and sends the resulting transaction lifecycle webhooks to Hookdeck Console.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: After a second test payment, Hookdeck Console displays incoming Paddle events, including the illustrated `transaction.completed` payload.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: After the second sandbox checkout, Hookdeck receives Paddle lifecycle events including `transaction.completed`.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the earliest observable terminal in the selected official quickstart. The workshop policy permits treating signup as one opaque step and then reconstructing the publicly documented post-login path.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a separate Paddle sandbox account even if a live account already exists.
Create Starter and Pro products with monthly and annual prices, then retain their price IDs.
Create and copy a sandbox client-side token for Paddle.js.
Set a default payment link; the sandbox guide permits any domain without verification, and the Quickstart permits `https://localhost/` when no website exists.
Use an owned email address, a supported country, a valid ZIP or postal code when required, and Paddle's published sandbox card values for the test checkout.

primary_path.action: Open the official end-to-end Paddle Quickstart.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide lists its five stages.

primary_path.action: Create a Paddle sandbox account as one opaque signup step.
primary_path.output: An authenticated Paddle sandbox dashboard is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Complete any signup, verification, legal, and business setup requested by Paddle; individual form fields are outside this record.

primary_path.action: Create Starter and Pro products, each with monthly and annual prices.
primary_path.success_signal: The products and prices appear under Catalog.

primary_path.action: Create and copy a sandbox client-side token under Developer tools > Authentication.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A live token does not match the sandbox environment.

primary_path.action: Put the token and four price IDs into the official getting-started CodePen.
primary_path.output: The pricing page loads localized prices.
primary_path.success_signal: Starter and Pro prices render and the billing toggle works.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Check token and `pri_` IDs if prices do not appear.

primary_path.action: Set the Default payment link under Checkout settings, using `https://localhost/` if no website exists.
primary_path.output: Paddle Checkout can generate required URLs.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Checkout may fail without this setting.

primary_path.action: Open checkout from CodePen and complete a sandbox payment with Paddle’s published test values.
primary_path.output: A test transaction is paid without real money.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use a supported country and valid test values.

primary_path.action: Open Hookdeck Console, copy its endpoint, then create a Paddle platform webhook destination for all events.
primary_path.output: Paddle has an enabled notification destination.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use the event endpoint, not the Hookdeck console URL.
Check Paddle notification logs if no events arrive.

primary_path.output: The end-to-end Paddle Quickstart is complete.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Queued or failed deliveries are not the terminal.

branches.condition: Choose a backend SDK or starter kit instead
branches.path: Follow the selected Node.js, Python, Go, PHP, Next.js, or web monetization start from Paddle Get started.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the implementation, credentials, and terminal from the framework-agnostic checkout-and-webhook route.

branches.path: Log in, create Starter and Pro products with monthly and annual prices, create a client-side token, put the token and four price IDs into the getting-started CodePen, verify localized prices, set a default payment link, and take a test payment.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Skips the blocked signup transition and reaches the checkout portion of the Quickstart.

branches.condition: Continue from the first test payment to the Quickstart terminal
branches.path: Open Hookdeck Console, copy its endpoint, create a platform webhook destination for all events, save it, run checkout again, and verify the lifecycle webhooks in Hookdeck.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Completes the guide's stated provisioning-webhook outcome after account access is available.

friction_gates.description: Paddle offers an end-to-end walkthrough, four backend SDK starts, AI-tool routes, and starter kits; this record follows the explicitly end-to-end Quickstart.
A separate Paddle sandbox account is required for testing.
The sandbox documentation requires personal and business details, but the current form is not publicly enumerable without JavaScript.
The downstream pricing page requires a sandbox client-side token and four price IDs.
Checkout requires a default payment link before the first test payment.
The Quickstart uses published test payment details in sandbox and no real money.

sources.title: Get started | Paddle Developer Docs
evidence_supported: Peer SDK, starter-kit, and AI-tool routes

sources.title: Paddle Login | Sign into your Paddle Account
evidence_supported: Current sandbox signup requires client-side JavaScript and exposes no reproducible fields or completion state

excluded_after_success: Production account verification, domain approval, live credentials, go-live migration, real payments, provisioning implementation, customer portal, Retain, refunds, and subscription lifecycle management occur after the sandbox webhook terminal.
